changedHeuristics: Prefer banishing challenges over questing when the challenge cuts meaningful opposing lore pressure.
Prefer board-developing plays over inking when the play uses current ink and keeps branching bounded.
candidateManifests.hypothesis: Board-control lore racing should gain mirror equity by contesting opposing lore engines earlier without collapsing tempo.
candidateManifests.notes: Use quick and candidate presets to validate same-deck gains first, then promotion to confirm full-fixture regression safety.

changedHeuristics: Reuse the current self-first and structural mulligan opening choices.
Challenge earlier when the opposing exerted character is worth a favorable or equal value trade.
Allow mutual-banish trades when they remove a higher-value opposing lore engine or board anchor.
candidateManifests.hypothesis: An aggressive board-control variant should outperform the current board-control profile in threat-dense mirrors by cashing in more value trades before the opponent snowballs lore.
candidateManifests.notes: Compare it directly against board-control-lore-race and default-lore-race on the quick preset before widening to promotion coverage.
